methods.description: Describes the process used to count laminae on a speleothem cross-section using high-resolution photography and manual verification.

steps.description: The speleothem used in the project was cut and polished by van Beynen.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare speleothem by cutting and polishing.

steps.description: First, a cross section of speleothem was laid on a black table to increase the contrast.
steps.searchable_summary: Place speleothem cross-section on a black table for imaging.

steps.description: Then, its photos were taken using a high-resolution camera, measuring the same distance between the speleothem c-axis and the camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Take high-resolution photos of the speleothem cross-section.

steps.description: High resolution prints of these photos (see Figure 1, right) were used to count the laminae.
steps.searchable_summary: Create high-resolution prints from photos for manual analysis.

steps.description: The counting was aided by a desk lamp, a magnifying glass, and a Sharpie marker (see Figure 1, left).
steps.searchable_summary: Manually count laminae on photo prints using aids.

steps.description: For comparison, laminae were marked in the center and the bottom of the images.
steps.searchable_summary: Mark laminae in specific areas (center, bottom) for comparison.
outputs: Photo prints with specific area markings

steps.description: Once the initial markings were made, the photos were given to Katherine Deberry for additional verification.
steps.searchable_summary: Verify laminae markings and counts by a second person.

methods.description: Describes the statistical analysis performed on the speleothem laminae counts, including calculating averages, fitting a least-squares line, and comparing results with external data.

steps.description: The averages of all the counts are presented in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the average of all laminae counts.

steps.description: We have calculated the leastsquares line of the best fit for these counts as where Here, stands for the total number of data points while and are the means of the distances and the laminae counts respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the least-squares line of best fit for laminae counts vs. distance.
outputs: Slope (m) and intercept (b) of the least-squares line

steps.description: We have used Microsoft Excel to confirm our findings (see Charts 1-5)
steps.searchable_summary: Confirm least-squares calculation using Microsoft Excel.

steps.description: and compared the results with van Beynen's radioisotope data (van Beynen).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare calculated results (least-squares line) with external radioisotope data.

steps.description: Unfortunately, our results did not correlate with this data well.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess and report the correlation between calculated results and external data.
